created_for: Assembler System canonical content editing and knowledge-base querying skill architecture

0.1_purpose.summary: Defines the release package for two portable Assembler System skills: a Canonical Content Editor Skill and a Rollup Query Expert Skill.
0.1_purpose.primary_goal: Separate skill intelligence from runtime integration by defining Shared Canonical Kernel, Skill Core, Canonical CLI Layer, Environment Adapter Layer, and Execution Contracts.
0.1_purpose.core_architecture_law: Core decides. CLI expresses. Adapter executes. Runtime hosts.
0.1_purpose.semantic_authority_law: The skills must not redefine the Assembler System canon. They must rely on the canonical release files from packages 00 through 06 as their semantic evidence base.
0.1_purpose.portability_law: Skill cores must remain portable across Codex, Kilo Code, OpenCode, GitHub, Google Drive, Local Runtime, and future environments through adapter contracts.

in_scope: source data manifests and refresh policy
shared canonical kernel for both skills
execution contracts between core, CLI, adapters, and runtimes
portable architecture for future integration expansion
out_of_scope: implementation code for a production CLI
final Python package or npm package
new Assembler System responsibilities or obligations
actual Codex, Kilo Code, or OpenCode plugin packaging
hardcoded dependency on external sibling workspace folders
runtime assumption that upstream source folders exist outside 08_assembler_skills

source_priority: Reference Markdown and schemas define human-readable canonical intent.
CSV indexes and matrices define flat queryable records.
Package 08 defines skill behavior and integration architecture only.
All source dependencies resolve through vendored 00_source_data/ paths.

1.0_source_data_snapshot.role: Vendored canonical source data used by the skills package.
1.0_source_data_snapshot.authority: Provides local source evidence copied or snapshotted from upstream canonical Assembler packages.
1.0_source_data_snapshot.must_not_do: Must not depend on external workspace-relative paths at runtime.

1.1_shared_canonical_kernel.role: Common governance substrate used by both skills.
1.1_shared_canonical_kernel.authority: Defines ID grammar, source priority, validation baseline, and canonical distinctions.
1.1_shared_canonical_kernel.must_not_do: Must not perform editing or querying as an operational actor by itself.

1.2_skill_core.role: Environment-independent reasoning layer for each skill.
1.2_skill_core.content_editor_core: Classifies canonical changes, performs impact analysis, generates patch plans, and validates propagation.
1.2_skill_core.query_expert_core: Routes queries, performs trace/join reasoning, validates evidence, and returns guarded answers.
1.2_skill_core.must_not_do: Must not contain environment-specific filesystem, repository, Drive, or terminal behavior.

1.3_canonical_cli_layer.role: Canonical operational interface between skill cores and runtime adapters.
1.3_canonical_cli_layer.core_rule: The CLI layer is not an adapter. It is the shared command grammar and command I/O model.

1.4_environment_adapter_layer.role: Runtime binding layer that translates canonical CLI command objects into environment-specific actions.
1.4_environment_adapter_layer.must_not_do: Adapters must not make canonical semantic decisions.

1.5_runtime_environment.role: External execution host such as repository, workspace, Drive folder, local filesystem, or coding agent environment.
1.5_runtime_environment.must_not_do: Runtime environment must not be treated as a semantic authority.

2.1_content_editor_skill.authority: May propose and generate canonical content updates only through impact analysis, propagation rules, validation, and review outputs.
primary_functions: generate updated Markdown, CSV, and JSON content
update manifests and counts when required
must_not_do: silently modify a canonical file without updating dependent indexes
introduce an ID without registering it in the appropriate index
promote governed artifacts into actors without explicit taxonomy update

2.2_query_expert_skill.authority: May query, trace, explain, validate, and navigate canonical records. Must not introduce new semantics.
primary_functions: route to the correct query family
join responsibility, obligation, entity, enablement, surface, contract, gap, doctrine, and taxonomy records
2.2_query_expert_skill.core_constraint: No new semantics. Only query, trace, explain, validate, and navigate.
must_not_do: answer without evidence when evidence is required

3.0_file_inventory.role: Binding architectural correction requiring vendored source data and forbidding hardcoded external workspace paths.

6.0_validation_rules.statement: Package 08 must not redefine Assembler System responsibilities, obligations, entities, governed objects, enablements, contracts, gaps, or doctrines.
Skill cores, CLI layers, adapters, and runtime environments must remain separate architectural layers.
The CLI layer is the canonical operational interface before adapters, not one adapter among many.
Adapters may read, write, patch, search, validate, or report depending on capability, but they must not decide canonical semantics.
The Content Editor Skill must not emit a canonical edit without impact analysis and propagation requirements.
The Query Expert Skill must remain read-only and must not generate canonical edits.
The Query Expert Skill must produce source/evidence paths for canonical answers when evidence is required.
Both skills must preserve all anti-collapse rules inherited from packages 01 through 06.
Skill cores must not contain Codex, Kilo Code, OpenCode, GitHub, Google Drive, or Local Runtime assumptions.
Every adapter must declare supported capabilities before executing commands.
All skill source dependencies must resolve through 08_assembler_skills/00_source_data/ or approved source-data aliases.

6.0_validation_rules.rule_name: No Hardcoded External Workspace Path Rule
6.0_validation_rules.statement: Package 08 files must not require external sibling workspace folders at runtime.

7.0_environment_targets.expected_role: repository-aware coding and patch execution environment
7.0_environment_targets.adapter_scope: branching, diff generation, file edits, validation command execution, review-ready patch outputs
workspace discovery, file patching, local validation, structured edit sessions

7.0_environment_targets.expected_role: terminal and local workspace-oriented agent environment
7.0_environment_targets.adapter_scope: filesystem access, shell execution, validation scripts, local diff and packaging

7.0_environment_targets.expected_role: repository and pull request integration environment
7.0_environment_targets.adapter_scope: repository file fetch/update, branch creation, commit generation, pull request creation, issue or review comment reporting

7.0_environment_targets.expected_role: Drive-backed document, sheet, and file source environment
7.0_environment_targets.adapter_scope: Drive file search, document/sheet fetch, content export, structured update where supported

7.0_environment_targets.expected_role: plain filesystem and CLI execution environment
7.0_environment_targets.adapter_scope: local file reads/writes, command execution, validation reports, artifact exports

9.0_final_canonical_statement.statement: The 08_assembler_skills package defines a portable two-skill architecture for maintaining and querying the Assembler System canon. It introduces no new Assembler System semantics. It defines how editor and query skill cores use vendored source data, a shared canonical kernel, expose canonical CLI commands, and delegate execution to environment adapters without allowing adapters or runtimes to become semantic authorities.
9.0_final_canonical_statement.canonical_operating_law: Source data is vendored. Kernel governs. Core reasons. CLI expresses. Adapter executes. Runtime hosts.
non_canonical_if: a skill core depends directly on a runtime-specific API
the CLI layer is treated as one adapter among many
the editor skill emits edits without propagation analysis
anti-collapse rules from the canonical release are weakened
evidence-required query answers omit source paths
it requires external sibling workspace paths at runtime
it omits 00_source_data/ while claiming portable operation
it treats a live runtime folder as semantic authority without snapshot policy
it allows adapters to rewrite source references without manifest trace
it allows query skill to read outside source-data aliases by default
it allows editor skill to mutate source data without propagation and validation
it treats vendored source data as implementation code rather than canonical evidence
it fails to record source-data refreshes
